# Contributing

When contributing to this repository, please first discuss the change you wish
to make via issue, email, or any other method with the owners of this repository
before making a change.

Please note we have a code of conduct, please follow it in all your interactions
with the project.

## Pull Request Process

1. Update the `README.md` with details of changes including example HCL blocks
   and [example files](./examples) if appropriate. The inputs/outputs table is
   auto-generated by `terraform-docs` — you do not need to edit it manually, but
   do keep `description` fields on all variables and outputs up to date.
2. Run pre-commit hooks locally: `pre-commit run --all-files`.
3. Ensure all CI checks pass on your PR.
4. Once all outstanding comments and checklist items have been addressed, your
   contribution will be merged. Merged PRs will be included in the next release.
   The terraform-oci-vcn maintainers take care of updating the CHANGELOG as they
   merge (via automated semantic-release).

## Local Development Requirements

Before contributing you need the following tools installed locally:

- `terraform` >= 1.5
- `pre-commit` — `pip3 install pre-commit`
- `tflint` v0.59.1 — see [DEVELOPMENT.md](../../DEVELOPMENT.md)
- `terraform-docs` v0.20.0 — see [DEVELOPMENT.md](../../DEVELOPMENT.md)
- `oci` CLI — for running examples against a real tenancy

After cloning, register the git hooks once:

```bash
pre-commit install
```

See [DEVELOPMENT.md](../../DEVELOPMENT.md) for the full setup guide including
OCI authentication and day-to-day workflow.

## Checklists for Contributions

- [ ] Add a [semantic prefix](#semantic-pull-requests) to your PR title
- [ ] CI checks are passing (pre-commit + pr-title validation)
- [ ] `README.md` inputs/outputs table reflects any variable/output changes
      (run `pre-commit run terraform_docs --all-files` to regenerate)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass locally: `pre-commit run --all-files`
- [ ] New examples or changed examples validate cleanly:
      `terraform -chdir=examples/<name> init -backend=false && terraform -chdir=examples/<name> validate`

## Semantic Pull Requests

To generate the changelog, Pull Requests or Commits must be semantic and follow
the Conventional Commits specification:

- `feat:` for new features — triggers a **minor** version release
- `fix:` for bug fixes — triggers a **patch** version release
- `feat!:` or a commit body containing `BREAKING CHANGE:` — triggers a **major**
  version release
- `docs:` for documentation and examples — no release triggered
- `refactor:` for code refactoring with no behaviour change — no release triggered
- `ci:` for CI/CD pipeline changes — no release triggered
- `chore:` for housekeeping (dependency bumps, etc.) — no release triggered

The subject (text after the colon) must start with an uppercase letter.

**Valid examples:**
```
feat: Add database subnet support
fix: Correct NAT gateway route table association on destroy
feat!: Rename vcn_name variable to name for AWS parity
docs: Add hub-spoke topology example
ci: Pin tflint to v0.59.1
chore: Update pre-commit hook versions to latest
```

**Invalid examples:**
```
add database subnets        (missing type prefix)
feat: add database subnets  (subject must start uppercase)
```

The `chore` prefix is skipped during changelog generation. Use it for commit
messages like `chore: update CHANGELOG` or dependency bumps.
